north_star: Beat the strongest public agent-memory systems with a reproducible benchmark-native architecture.

benchmarks.reported_result: 95.60% overall with Chronos High; 92.60% overall with Chronos Low
benchmarks.win_condition: Beat the best publicly sourced overall score with a reproducible run.
benchmarks.notes: Chronos, submitted on March 17, 2026, is the highest public LongMemEvalS claim in the current source sweep. Mastra OM remains the strongest implementation-backed public claim at 94.87% with gpt-5-mini and 84.23% with gpt-4o.
benchmarks.next_action: Treat Chronos as the frontier score target and Mastra OM as the strongest implementation-backed comparison point during reproduction.

benchmarks.reported_result: #1 claim, exact public score not pinned in reviewed source
benchmarks.win_condition: Pin exact sourced threshold, then beat it on the same task definition.
benchmarks.notes: Public README claims #1, but exact numeric score was not visible in the source sweep.
benchmarks.next_action: Capture exact leaderboard number from official benchmark or reproducible MemoryBench run.

benchmarks.dataset_scope: published benchmark configurations from 4k to 500k context spans
benchmarks.reported_result: Living benchmark with published configs and reported GoodAI agent baselines; exact single frontier number depends on configuration.
benchmarks.win_condition: Beat strong baselines inside the published harness on the chosen configuration set and preserve reproducibility.
benchmarks.notes: Best used as an internal core stress benchmark for long-span conversational memory and continual-memory upkeep rather than as a single public leaderboard scalar.
benchmarks.next_action: Choose the first canonical configuration set, reproduce the published baseline path, and lock the scorecard contract.

benchmarks.dataset_scope: 100 coherent conversations and 2,000 validated questions up to 10M tokens
benchmarks.reported_result: Paper reports LIGHT improving 3.5% to 12.69% over the strongest baselines depending on the backbone LLM.
benchmarks.win_condition: Reproduce the benchmark definition faithfully once code or data access is pinned, then beat the strongest reported baseline on the chosen slice.
benchmarks.notes: High-value frontier benchmark for higher-context and million-token-scale memory pressure. Current source is the paper; implementation surface still needs pinning.
benchmarks.next_action: Track public code or dataset release, then lock the benchmark adapter and scorecard contract.

experimental_frontier_claims.reported_result: ~98.60% with an 8-variant ensemble; ~97.20% with a 12-variant decision forest; framed as ~99% frontier memory
experimental_frontier_claims.notes: User-provided Supermemory ASMR writeup describes a forthcoming experimental release rather than the main production engine. The architecture uses multi-agent ingestion, agentic search, and answer ensembles. Treat it as a high-signal frontier claim to learn from, not yet as a pinned reproducible benchmark bar.
experimental_frontier_claims.next_action: Track the promised public release, pin the exact implementation surface, then reproduce against the same LongMemEval_s path.

shadow_benchmarks.scope: 75,336 QA pairs across user facts, assistant facts, preferences, changing facts, implicit connections, and abstention
shadow_benchmarks.why: Guardrail against overbuilding retrieval when full context still works and against regressions on preferences, changing facts, and abstention.

canonical_configs.rationale: Program inference: 32k is the first serious long-span setting that remains practical for early reproducible baseline loops. 1k is too weak, while 120k+ is better suited for later stress promotion.

architecture_bets: stable observational compression for very large context windows
lightweight online path with offline consolidation

active_optimization_lane.why: Current best measured path in-repo; reached a perfect real rerun on the first 50 LongMemEval_s samples and remains materially ahead of the current beam comparison checkpoint.

ten_system_variants.name: Full-Context Control
ten_system_variants.benchmark_posture: use as an honesty baseline, not as the target architecture

ten_system_variants.name: Lexical Retrieval Memory
ten_system_variants.benchmark_posture: cheap baseline that should lose once temporal pressure rises

ten_system_variants.name: Vector Semantic Retrieval Memory
ten_system_variants.benchmark_posture: common production baseline, but weak on stale facts and time

ten_system_variants.name: Rolling Summary Memory
ten_system_variants.benchmark_posture: lightweight but vulnerable to irreversible information loss

ten_system_variants.name: Temporal Atom Memory
ten_system_variants.benchmark_posture: strong benchmark-native default for updates and supersession

ten_system_variants.name: Event Calendar Memory
ten_system_variants.benchmark_posture: high-upside for temporal and multi-hop slices

ten_system_variants.name: Observational Stable-Window Memory
ten_system_variants.benchmark_posture: important for BEAM-style pressure and stable prompt caching

ten_system_variants.name: Agentic Search Memory
ten_system_variants.benchmark_posture: frontier claim lane only until lighter systems plateau

ten_system_variants.name: Relation Graph Memory
ten_system_variants.benchmark_posture: second-wave path for multi-hop and entity-heavy slices

ten_system_variants.name: Dual-Store Consolidated Memory
ten_system_variants.benchmark_posture: best long-range candidate once BEAM pressure starts to dominate

initial_system_ladder.name: Beam-Ready Temporal Atom Router
initial_system_ladder.purpose: Lightweight default build for LongMemEval, LoCoMo, GoodAI, and early BEAM pressure.

initial_system_ladder.name: Observational Temporal Memory
initial_system_ladder.purpose: Stable-window compressed memory candidate for large-context and BEAM-oriented runs.

initial_system_ladder.name: Dual-Store Event Calendar Hybrid
initial_system_ladder.purpose: Highest-upside hybrid once the first two systems define the best lightweight ingredients.

remaining_work: promotion and rollback logging over real runs
combination_search_doctrine: Start with a strong lightweight baseline before trying heavier combinations.
Add one component family at a time and compare against the direct parent baseline.
Prefer combinations that pair compact retrieval units with temporal disambiguation and selective routing.
Treat heavyweight online combinations as guilty until they beat the lightweight stack clearly.

candidate_combinations.why: Strong simple retrieval baseline with raw provenance.
Most defensible V1 core for changing facts and temporal reasoning.
Adds selective routing without a heavyweight online path.
Best current candidate for a serious benchmark-ready lightweight stack.

skeptical_combinations.risk: retrieval complexity explosion before the lightweight baseline is exhausted

priority_mutations.rationale: Public frontier is most differentiated on knowledge updates and temporal reasoning.
priority_mutations.comparison_rule: Compare against semantic-atom retrieval without temporal logic.
priority_mutations.keep_if: Keep only if knowledge-update improves without a broad regression elsewhere.

priority_mutations.rationale: The strongest lightweight path is likely semantic atoms plus time-aware routing, not raw search complexity.
priority_mutations.comparison_rule: Compare against the same atom-plus-time system with no query-aware routing.
priority_mutations.keep_if: Keep only if it improves LongMemEval and does not regress on short-history shadow slices where full context should still be strong.

priority_mutations.rationale: The shadow benchmark and LongMemEval both punish over-answering; abstention should be optimized explicitly.
priority_mutations.comparison_rule: Compare against the same retrieval stack without explicit abstention calibration.
priority_mutations.keep_if: Keep only if abstention improves without suppressing too many correct answers.

priority_mutations.rationale: Relation expansion is one of the best second-wave additions once the temporal-semantic core is stable.
priority_mutations.comparison_rule: Compare against the same stack with relation expansion disabled.
priority_mutations.keep_if: Keep only if multi-hop and temporal LoCoMo slices rise enough to justify the extra retrieval work.

promotion_rule: Do not promote a doctrine until it improves benchmark behavior and survives contradiction review.
